In the promise itself we may consider, 1. Whom it is made unto, 2. What it is that is promised:—

Τὸ δέ . There are in the words, (1.) The notation of the subject, τὸ δέ ,—"but that," or 'that, whatever it be.' The general rule gives evidence unto the former inference, 'Whatever it be that waxeth old.'
In the promise itself we may consider, 1. Whom it is made unto, 2. What it is that is promised:—

Τὸ δέ . There are in the words, (1.) The notation of the subject, τὸ δέ ,—"but that," or 'that, whatever it be.' The general rule gives evidence unto the former inference, 'Whatever it be that waxeth old.'
